title: Goldfish Growth
description: How large can a goldfish grow and what factors affect its size?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on the typical maximum size of goldfish, including variations among breeds, and the environmental factors (e.g., tank or pond size, water volume, nutrition) that influence growth. Relevant documents should mention specific length ranges (e.g., up to 12 inches or more), growth requirements such as minimum water volume per inch of fish, and recommendations for appropriate habitats.

title: Wi-Fi vs Bluetooth
description: What are the key differences between Wi‑Fi (including Wi‑Fi Direct) and Bluetooth technologies?
narrative: The user wants a side‑by‑side comparison of Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth, covering technical characteristics such as data transfer speed, range, underlying standards, and typical use cases (e.g., file sharing, audio streaming). Relevant documents should provide factual information on performance metrics (e.g., 250 Mbps vs. 25 Mbps, 200 ft vs. 30 ft), explain how each technology works, and highlight the pros and cons of each for different scenarios.

title: US WWI Entry
description: Why did the United States voluntarily enter World War I?
narrative: The user wants a concise historical explanation of the motivations behind the United States’ decision to declare war on Germany in 1917, covering political, economic, and diplomatic factors such as unrestricted submarine warfare, the Lusitania incident, the Zimmermann Telegram, and related trade concerns, as well as scholarly interpretations of these causes.

title: Declaratory Judgment Definition
description: What is a declaratory judgment and in what circumstances is it used?
narrative: The user seeks a clear, authoritative legal definition of a declaratory judgment, including its purpose, the types of disputes it addresses, and the procedural context in which courts may issue it. Relevant documents should explain that a declaratory judgment determines the parties' rights or legal status without ordering any specific action, describe its role in clarifying law or constitutionality, and contrast it with other remedies such as consent or summary judgments.

title: Right Pelvic Pain
description: What are the possible medical causes of right pelvic pain?
narrative: The user is seeking information on the various conditions that can produce pain in the right pelvic region, such as kidney stones, appendicitis, ovarian cysts, and other abdominal or gynecological disorders. Relevant documents should describe specific causes, associated symptoms, and any diagnostic clues for right pelvic pain.

title: Social Determinants
description: Identify the key social determinants of health and explain how they influence health outcomes.
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of the social determinants of health, including a clear definition, enumeration of major factors (e.g., socioeconomic status, education, neighborhood, employment, access to care, social support, etc.), and an explanation of how these conditions affect health status, functioning, and quality of life. Relevant content should describe the concept, list the determinants, and discuss their relevance for policy and practice.

title: Legionella Pneumonia
description: Does Legionella pneumophila cause pneumonia and what are the clinical features of that infection?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative information confirming whether the bacterium Legionella pneumophila is a causative agent of pneumonia (specifically Legionnaires' disease) and seeks details on the disease’s presentation, severity, and differentiation from related conditions such as Pontiac fever and other atypical pneumonias. Relevant documents should explicitly state that Legionella pneumophila causes Legionnaires' disease, describe it as a severe form of pneumonia, outline typical symptom progression, and note its classification as an atypical pneumonia pathogen.

title: Jamaica Weather
description: What are the current weather conditions and typical climate characteristics for Jamaica?
narrative: The user is seeking information about Jamaica's weather, including temperature, precipitation, humidity, sunshine, sea temperature, and UV index. Relevant material should contain recent forecasts or monthly climate summaries that describe these elements for Jamaica (or specific locations within the country).

title: CP Dysarthria Types
description: What are the different types of dysarthria that can occur in individuals with cerebral palsy?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive list and brief description of the dysarthria subtypes associated with cerebral palsy (e.g., spastic, flaccid, ataxic, mixed), including characteristic speech features and any relevant clinical distinctions. Relevant documents should explicitly name and explain these types.

title: Robert Gray Biography
description: Who was Robert Gray and what were his notable achievements?
narrative: The user seeks biographical information about the early American sea captain Robert Gray, focusing on his life, his voyages to the Northwest coast (including the 1787 and 1790 expeditions), his discovery of the Columbia River in 1792, and his role in early Pacific Northwest trade and U.S. territorial claims. Relevant material may also mention related historic sites, vessels (e.g., Lady Washington, Columbia Rediviva), and his lasting historical significance.

title: Sous Vide Foods
description: What types of food can be prepared using the sous‑vide cooking method?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview of food categories that are suitable for sous‑vide cooking, such as meats, poultry, fish, seafood, eggs, vegetables, fruits, and desserts, along with typical examples or recipes. Relevant documents should explicitly mention these food types, describe how they are cooked sous‑vide, and may provide tips or temperature ranges. Documents that only discuss the technique without naming specific foods, or that focus solely on equipment, are not relevant.

title: Flea Life Cycle
description: What is the total duration of a flea's life cycle from egg to death?
narrative: The user is seeking a concise estimate of how long a flea lives through all developmental stages, including egg, larva, pupa, and adult phases. Relevant information should state the typical range (e.g., two weeks to two years) and note common durations such as adult lifespan of two to three months, while also mentioning factors that affect timing, such as temperature, humidity, and availability of a blood meal.

title: Contour Plowing Benefits
description: Which types of environmental degradation can contour plowing help reduce?
narrative: The user wants to know the specific negative impacts that contour plowing mitigates. Relevant material should describe how contour plowing reduces soil erosion, topsoil loss, runoff velocity, seed displacement, and related water‑related damage, and may provide explanations or evidence of these reductions compared with straight‑up‑and‑down slope plowing.

title: Salvation Army Founding
description: When was The Salvation Army founded?
narrative: The user wants the precise year (and any supporting date) that The Salvation Army was established, including reference to its founder and early history; documents providing the founding year, date, founder name, and contextual background are relevant.

title: Active Margin Definition
description: What is an active margin and how does it differ from a passive margin?
narrative: The user seeks a clear geological definition of an active margin, including its tectonic setting (e.g., a plate boundary where an oceanic plate subducts beneath a continental plate), typical features such as frequent earthquakes, volcanoes, mountain building, and examples like the west coast of South America. Relevant documents should explicitly define active margins, contrast them with passive margins, and describe associated processes and real‑world locations.

title: RN vs BSN
description: What are the main differences between a Registered Nurse (RN) and a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) regarding education, scope of practice, career options, and compensation?
narrative: The user wants a comparative overview of RN and BSN qualifications, focusing on required education, licensure, job responsibilities, advancement opportunities, and salary differences. Relevant documents should explicitly contrast RN and BSN roles, describe RN‑to‑BSN pathways, and highlight how a BSN expands career prospects compared to a diploma or associate RN.

title: Medicare Ventilation Definition
description: What is the official Medicare definition of mechanical ventilation?
narrative: The user is looking for the precise definition of "mechanical ventilation" as used by Medicare for coverage and billing purposes. Relevant documents should include Medicare (CMS) policy language, coverage manuals, or HCPCS/CPT coding guidance that describe the criteria (e.g., invasive vs. non‑invasive, patient effort, ventilation settings, duration thresholds) that qualify a procedure as mechanical ventilation under Medicare rules. Sources that merely explain the clinical use of ventilators or unrelated respiratory therapies are not relevant.

title: Trapezoid Midsegment
description: How do I find the midsegment (median) of a trapezoid?
narrative: The user wants instructional information that explains how to locate and compute the midsegment of a trapezoid. Relevant material should define the trapezoid median, describe how to identify the midpoints of the non‑parallel sides, and provide the formula or method for calculating the midsegment length (typically the average of the lengths of the two bases). Sources that include step‑by‑step procedures, geometric reasoning, or illustrative examples are considered relevant.

title: AML Surveillance Analyst
description: What are the duties, responsibilities, and qualifications of an AML surveillance analyst?
narrative: The user wants a clear definition and overview of the AML surveillance analyst role, including typical tasks such as monitoring suspicious transactions, reporting, and supporting AML compliance, as well as required skills and how the position fits into anti‑money‑laundering efforts. Relevant passages should describe the job functions, responsibilities, and context within AML operations.

title: Thai Daily Life
description: What are the typical daily routines, cultural practices, and everyday experiences of people living in Thailand?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview of everyday life for Thai people, including work habits, family and social interactions, common activities, food customs, transportation, and cultural observances such as holidays and language use. Relevant documents should describe ordinary practices and cultural norms that shape the day‑to‑day existence of Thai citizens, rather than merely factual information about currency or tourism.

title: SIGMET Definition
description: What is a SIGMET and how is it defined in aviation weather reporting?
narrative: The user seeks an authoritative definition of a SIGMET, including its purpose, issuing authority, and the hazardous weather conditions it addresses; relevant documents should explicitly state what a SIGMET is and may compare it to related notices such as AIRMET.

title: Concrete Flooring Cost
description: What are the typical costs per square foot for installing interior concrete flooring, including basic pour and finish and any additional preparation or decorative charges?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed pricing information for interior concrete flooring projects. Relevant content should contain per‑square‑foot cost ranges for a basic pour and finish, as well as factors that can increase the price such as surface preparation, crack or spall repair, and decorative finishes. Documents that provide concrete cost estimates and explain how floor size or complexity influences the total expense are considered relevant.

title: Swiss Food Popularity
description: What is the most popular food eaten in Switzerland?
narrative: The user wants to know which food item is most commonly consumed or most culturally iconic in Switzerland. Relevant documents should describe foods that are widely eaten or strongly associated with Swiss cuisine, such as Swiss chocolate, cheese, traditional breakfast items, or other staple dishes. Sources that mention popularity, frequency of consumption, or cultural prominence of a particular food will satisfy the need.

title: Shark Endothermy
description: Which shark species are warm‑blooded and what physiological mechanisms allow them to maintain higher body temperatures?
narrative: The user seeks information on sharks that exhibit partial endothermy, including specific species (e.g., mako, great white, thresher, porbeagle) and the biological adaptations—such as the rete mirabile and heat‑conserving circulatory systems—that enable elevated internal temperatures. Relevant documents should explain why most sharks are ectothermic, describe the heat‑conserving mechanisms, and identify the species that possess them.

title: Durable Medical Equipment
description: What items are included in durable medical equipment (DME) and how are they defined?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive description of the categories and specific examples of durable medical equipment. Relevant documents should define DME, list typical products (e.g., wheelchairs, hospital beds, oxygen equipment, crutches, blood‑testing supplies, prosthetics, orthotics, white canes), and explain the context of home or extended‑use medical supplies. Materials that provide a clear inventory or taxonomy of DME items and the criteria for classifying an item as durable medical equipment are considered relevant.

title: Exon Definition
description: What is an exon and how is it defined in biological terms?
narrative: The user seeks a clear, concise definition of an exon, including its role as a coding segment of a gene that remains in the mature RNA after introns are removed by splicing. Relevant documents should explain the DNA and RNA context of exons, their relationship to introns, and their function in producing mature messenger RNA.

title: Visceral Definition
description: What is the meaning of the word “visceral,” particularly when used to describe feelings, gut reactions, or instinctive responses?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear, authoritative definition of “visceral,” including its common usage to denote deep, instinctive or gut‑level emotions and any relevant physiological explanations (e.g., the role of the vagus nerve). Documents that discuss visceral feelings as emotional intuitions, differentiate visceral from cerebral emotional reactions, or provide examples of gut‑instinct usage are considered relevant.

title: Tracheid Placement
description: What plant tissue or vascular system are tracheids a component of?
narrative: The user is seeking the specific vascular tissue that contains tracheids, i.e., the answer to the fill‑in‑the‑blank 'tracheids are part of _____'. Relevant passages should explicitly state that tracheids belong to the xylem (or a related vascular component) of plants.

title: RSA Key Definition
description: What is RSA and how are its public and private keys defined and used?
narrative: The user seeks a clear definition of the RSA cryptosystem together with an explanation of the key pair concept, describing the public encryption key and the private decryption key and their roles in secure data transmission. Relevant documents should provide a concise description of RSA and detail the asymmetry between the public and private keys.

title: CIS Formation
description: Who were the founders of the Commonwealth of Independent States?
narrative: The user wants to know which individuals, leaders, or states were instrumental in creating the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS). Relevant documents should mention the founding members, the key political figures (e.g., presidents or prime ministers) who signed the agreement, the date of formation, and the circumstances surrounding the establishment of the CIS after the dissolution of the Soviet Union.

title: LVH Causes
description: What are the underlying causes and risk factors that lead to left ventricular hypertrophy?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of medical conditions, physiological stresses, and lifestyle factors that can cause the left ventricle to enlarge and thicken. Relevant content should list and briefly explain each cause (e.g., chronic hypertension, aortic stenosis, mitral regurgitation, genetic cardiomyopathies, intense athletic training, obesity, diabetes, etc.) and describe how the increased workload or altered hemodynamics leads to hypertrophic remodeling of the left ventricle.

title: LPS Law Definition
description: What is the definition and scope of LPS laws (Lanterman‑Petris‑Short Act)?
narrative: The user wants a clear, concise explanation of LPS laws, including their purpose, main provisions, and how they govern involuntary psychiatric commitment. Relevant documents should define the LPS Act, outline its key elements (e.g., criteria for hospitalization, patient rights, procedural guidelines), and differentiate it from other mental‑health statutes such as the Florida Baker Act.

title: Three Percenters Overview
description: What is the Three Percenters movement, including its origins, beliefs, and activities?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of the Three Percenters, a U.S. extremist militia group. Relevant documents should describe the group's history, the origin of its name, core ideology, notable events, and its connections to other anti‑government groups such as Oath Keepers. Sources that provide factual definitions, background information, and contextual analysis of the movement’s goals and actions are considered relevant.

title: Military Suicide Causes
description: What are the primary factors that contribute to suicide among military personnel and veterans?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the underlying causes of suicide within the U.S. military and veteran populations. Relevant material should identify and explain psychological (e.g., PTSD, depression, substance abuse), social (e.g., isolation, stigma, family stress), occupational (e.g., combat exposure, deployment cycles, transition to civilian life), and demographic or cultural factors (e.g., gender, sexual orientation) that increase risk, as well as any statistical trends by service branch. Documents focusing solely on prevention programs or individual case stories without discussing causative factors are not relevant.

title: Theraderm Usage
description: What are the intended uses and applications of Theraderm skin‑care products?
narrative: The user wants to understand the purpose of Theraderm, including the skin concerns it addresses (e.g., anti‑aging, acne, sun damage), the types of products in the line, and how they are meant to be used. Relevant documents should describe the product system, its claimed benefits, and the contexts in which it is recommended.

title: Famvir Uses
description: What medical conditions is the antiviral drug Famvir (famciclovir) prescribed to treat?
narrative: The user wants to know the therapeutic indications for Famvir, i.e., the diseases or infections for which famciclovir is recommended (such as shingles, genital herpes, etc.). Relevant documents should explicitly state the conditions treated with Famvir, describe its role in therapy, or provide clinical guidance on its use. Sources that merely mention the drug name without linking it to a specific prescription purpose are not considered relevant.

title: Anthropological Environment Definition
description: What is the definition of “environment” according to anthropology?
narrative: The user wants a scholarly, anthropological definition of environment, focusing on how the concept is framed within cultural, ecological, and evolutionary anthropology. Relevant material should describe environment as it relates to human societies, subsistence modes, cultural adaptation, and ecological populations, and differentiate this view from purely biological or physical definitions.

title: Axon Terminal Definition
description: What is the definition and function of an axon terminal (synaptic knob) in a neuron?
narrative: The user seeks a clear, concise definition of an axon terminal, also known as a synaptic knob, including its anatomical location at the end of an axon, its role in forming synaptic connections with dendrites or effector cells, and its function in storing and releasing neurotransmitters. Relevant information should describe the structure, its relationship to other neuronal parts, and its involvement in neurotransmission.

title: CDG Main Airport
description: Is Charles de Gaulle (CDG) the primary airport serving Paris?
narrative: The user wants to confirm whether CDG is the main airport for Paris, looking for authoritative information that identifies CDG as the principal international gateway to the city, possibly mentioning its status relative to other Paris airports (e.g., Orly, Beauvais). Relevant documents should state that Charles de Gaulle is the main or primary airport for Paris, include its IATA code CDG, and may note its traffic volume or role as the primary hub for international flights.

title: Monotonic Function Example
description: What are some concrete examples of monotonic (increasing or decreasing) functions?
narrative: The user wants clear, illustrative examples of monotonic functions, including both strictly increasing and strictly decreasing cases. Relevant documents should define monotonicity, show simple mathematical functions such as f(x)=x, f(x)=2x+3, f(x)=e^x, f(x)=–x, and possibly non‑linear examples like f(x)=log(x) or piecewise linear functions, and explain why each preserves or reverses order over its domain.

title: Spruce Physical Description
description: What are the physical characteristics of a spruce tree?
narrative: The user seeks a botanical description of spruce trees, including overall size, habit, needle morphology, branch arrangement, cone type, and distinguishing features that separate spruce from other conifers. Relevant content should detail these observable traits.

title: Hydrogen Liquefaction Temperature
description: What is the temperature at which hydrogen transitions from gas to liquid under standard pressure?
narrative: The user wants a precise quantitative value (in °F or °C) for the boiling point of hydrogen at 1 atm, including any necessary context such as proximity to absolute zero and how it differs from the solidification point. Relevant documents should state the temperature at which hydrogen becomes a liquid, specify the pressure condition (standard pressure), and preferably provide both Fahrenheit and Celsius values. References to the temperature at which hydrogen solidifies are acceptable only as ancillary information; the primary relevance criterion is the liquefaction temperature.

title: McDouble vs Double
description: What are the differences between a McDonald’s McDouble and a Double Cheeseburger?
narrative: The user wants a detailed comparison of the two sandwich variants, including ingredients (number of patties, cheese slices), meat quality, taste differences, nutritional information (calories, fat, protein, carbs), and price range. Relevant documents should explicitly mention any of these aspects that distinguish a McDouble from a Double Cheeseburger.
